description: A plain engine with no colliding lifecycle-annotated cell does not fire E-TYPE-LIFECYCLE-ON-ENGINE-CELL (§14.12.4 / §51.0). Dropping the `<phase>: (.Idle to .Active)` declaration leaves the `<engine for=Phase …>` as the sole progression mechanism for the `phase` cell, so the lifecycle-on-engine check stays silent. The clean twin of e-type-lifecycle-on-engine-cell-pos.
rationale: §14.12.4 / §51.0 an engine cell driven only by rule= / initial= is well-formed; E-TYPE-LIFECYCLE-ON-ENGINE-CELL fires only when a lifecycle annotation is layered onto the engine cell.
